qa_pairs.question: Where might archived files that automatically save extended attributes typically originate from on compute clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: If archived files are coming from a Lustre filesystem, typically in `/home`, `/project`, or `/scratch` on general-purpose compute clusters, some extended attributes are saved automatically.

qa_pairs.question: How can I view the extended attributes assigned to each archived file?
qa_pairs.answer: To see which extended attributes are assigned to each archived file, use the `-alist-ea` flag with the `dar -l` command.

qa_pairs.question: What type of filesystem is associated with the automatic saving of extended attributes when archiving?
qa_pairs.answer: The Lustre filesystem is associated with the automatic saving of extended attributes when archiving files.

qa_pairs.question: On which directories on general-purpose compute clusters might archived files from a Lustre filesystem be found?
qa_pairs.answer: Archived files from a Lustre filesystem are typically found in `/home`, `/project`, or `/scratch` on general-purpose compute clusters.
